meta.description: Meta behavioral interview questions organized by level and Meta core values

meta_core_values: Be Direct and Respect Your Colleagues
Meta, Pair, Me (prioritize company > team > self)

questions.question: Tell me about a project you're most proud of. What was your role and what impact did it have?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Clear ownership, quantified impact, thoughtful reflection on what worked and what didn't
evaluation_rubric.weak: Vague contributions, no measurable outcomes, can't articulate personal role vs team effort

questions.question: Tell me about a time you had a disagreement with a teammate or manager. How did you handle it?
what_interviewer_looks_for: willingness to compromise or escalate appropriately
follow_ups: Would you handle it differently now?
How did this affect the relationship?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Shows empathy, seeks to understand the other perspective, focuses on the best outcome for the team/project
evaluation_rubric.weak: Blames others, avoids conflict entirely, or escalates without attempting resolution

questions.question: Tell me about a time you failed. What happened and what did you learn?
follow_ups: How did you apply what you learned?
How did you communicate the failure to stakeholders?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Genuine failure (not a humble brag), clear analysis of what went wrong, specific actions taken to prevent recurrence
evaluation_rubric.weak: Trivial failure, no real self-reflection, blames external factors

questions.question: Tell me about a time you had to make a decision with incomplete information. What did you do?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Made a reasoned decision quickly, identified key risks, had a plan to course-correct
evaluation_rubric.weak: Paralyzed by lack of information, or acted recklessly without considering risks

questions.question: Tell me about a time you drove a significant technical decision for your team or organization.
follow_ups: How did it turn out long-term?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Clear technical reasoning, considered multiple alternatives, built consensus, measured outcomes
evaluation_rubric.weak: Imposed decision without buy-in, shallow technical reasoning, no follow-through

questions.question: Tell me about a project that required working across multiple teams. How did you coordinate?
follow_ups: How did you handle misaligned priorities?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Proactively identified dependencies, aligned stakeholders, handled conflicts constructively
evaluation_rubric.weak: Waited for others to coordinate, blamed other teams for delays

questions.question: Tell me about a time you mentored someone. What was your approach and what was the outcome?
follow_ups: How did you adapt your style?
What did you learn from the experience?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Tailored mentoring approach, measurable growth in mentee, continued investment
evaluation_rubric.weak: Generic advice, no follow-through, can't describe specific impact on mentee

questions.question: Tell me about a time you identified an opportunity that others hadn't noticed. How did you act on it?
follow_ups: How did you prioritize this against existing work?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Identified genuine opportunity, built a case, executed to measurable impact
evaluation_rubric.weak: Opportunity was obvious, didn't follow through, or impact was trivial

questions.question: Tell me about a time you had to deliver under a tight deadline. How did you manage it?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Clear prioritization, proactive communication about trade-offs, delivered core value on time
evaluation_rubric.weak: Worked unsustainably, didn't communicate, or delivered poor quality

questions.question: Tell me about a time you influenced your organization's technical direction or strategy.
what_interviewer_looks_for: alignment of technical and business goals
follow_ups: How did you measure success at the org level?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Clear vision connected to business goals, built broad alignment, measurable org-level impact
evaluation_rubric.weak: Narrow technical scope, didn't connect to business value, couldn't build consensus

questions.question: Tell me about a time you had to quickly learn a new technology or domain to deliver a project.
follow_ups: How did you know when you knew enough to act?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Structured learning approach, leveraged experts, delivered successfully despite the ramp-up
evaluation_rubric.weak: Struggled without asking for help, or claimed expertise too quickly

questions.question: Tell me about a time you built or significantly grew a team. What was your approach to hiring, culture, and technical excellence?
follow_ups: How did you maintain quality as the team grew?

evaluation_rubric.strong: Clear team-building philosophy, high hiring bar, invested in culture and growth
evaluation_rubric.weak: Focused only on headcount, no culture or growth strategy
